text: Strongly pro-life, Karol Nawrocki, narrowly won the Polish presidential election on Monday, securing 50.89% of the vote in the race against pro-abortion Warsaw Mayor Rafał Trzaskowski. Nawrocki’s victory signals a shift in favour of pro-life policies, following an election campaign where the issue was front and centre. The push to overturn Poland’s pro-life laws has undoubtedly suffered a major setback following Nawrocki’s win, and the victory has also dealt a blow to the standing of Poland’s Prime Minister Donald Tusk, who is an ardent pro-abortion supporter. Pro-life advocates in Poland have reason to celebrate; however, efforts to expand abortion access are unlikely to subside. It is expected that Prime Minister Tusk and others will seek to apply pressure - both domestically and from the EU - to alter Poland’s current laws. In response, pro-life supporters must stay alert and continue proactive efforts to defend existing protections. #ProLife #News #Politics #PolishPolitics #Elections

text: @MrMireaux @poselTTrela The debate over a recount in the 2025 Polish presidential election stems from a close 50.89% to 49.11% victory for Karol Nawrocki over Rafał Trzaskowski, a margin of 369,591 votes. Concerns include 189,000 invalid votes and specific irregularities, like swapped results in one commission, backed by a petition with over 160,000 signatures. However, official results were declared, and no widespread fraud has been proven. The Supreme Court will decide after reviewing protests by June 16, 2025. Both sides have valid points, but the legal process must resolve this. grok.com

text: Karol Nawrocki został wybrany prezydentem Polski w 2025 roku, zdobywając 50,9% głosów w drugiej turze wyborów 1 czerwca, pokonując Rafała Trzaskowskiego (49,1%), według oficjalnych wyników PKW. Brak ostatecznych dowodów podważających legalność, ale sprawa pozostaje sporna.

text: Karol Nawrocki a été élu président de la République de Pologne. Un conservateur assumé, au discours patriote, aux accents souverainistes, plutôt critique à l’égard de Bruxelles. Cette victoire avait fait naître un certain espoir — en Pologne comme ailleurs — celui d’un possible rééquilibrage, d’un retour à une forme de dignité nationale, voire d’une inflexion dans la dynamique technocratique imposée depuis le centre européen. Mais très vite, comme trop souvent, la réalité s’est rappelée à nous. Et la douche froide n’a pas tardé. Donald Tusk, le Premier ministre, a été conforté par un vote de confiance. Il garde la majorité, les rouages du pouvoir, les leviers exécutifs. C’est lui qui gouverne. Lui qui décide. Lui qui structure déjà les prochaines réformes prévues dès juillet. Le président, nouvellement élu, devra cohabiter — mais sans gouverner. Il devient un personnage secondaire dans une pièce dont les actes sont déjà écrits. Tout cela montre, une fois encore, la duplicité profonde des effets d’annonce. L’élection présidentielle, grand-messe démocratique, donne l’illusion du choix. Mais le pouvoir réel reste ailleurs. Le peuple espère un tournant ; on lui livre un trompe-l’œil. Et ceux qui, sincèrement, veulent croire qu’une alternance est encore possible dans les urnes — voient leurs attentes réduites à une simple parenthèse narrative. La construction européenne, elle, peut dormir tranquille. Tusk est là. La Commission sait qu’elle pourra continuer à s’appuyer sur lui. Quant à Nawrocki, s’il venait à gêner un peu trop, tout est déjà prêt pour le neutraliser politiquement. Ce n’est pas une victoire conservatrice : c’est une opération de containment. Et une leçon, peut-être, sur ce qu’il reste des démocraties parlementaires quand le pouvoir s’organise ailleurs, en amont, à l’abri des bulletins de vote.

text: POLAND PRESIDENTIAL ELECTION: HOW BETTORS 3X'D IN AN HOUR Karol Nawroki defeated Rafał Trzaskowski by 1.7%. The election saw meteoric swings on the day of the election, prompted by virtually nothing. Before a single vote was counted, Nawroki—a nationalist, patriotic conservative (who, again, ended up winning by over 1.5%)—saw his odds of winning drop below 15%. Meaning, if you bought Nawroki shares on the day of the election, you could have made a 550% return in just one hour! With $130M in liquidity, there was plenty of profit for those who predicted the right outcome. But why was the market so wrong? The fall of Nawroki to 15c is a concept insiders call momentum trading. Think about it this way: If you see an election expiring in 1 hour and one person has a 75% chance to win, your natural inclination is to toss money on the likely winner—since the market is usually right—and collect a quick profit. This then causes a snowball effect, which can become extreme, pushing a 60-40 race to 85-15. The crazy part? We gave Nawroki, not Trzaskowski a 60% chance to win the night before. He polled better in Eastern Poland than Duda did five years ago in his re-election. He underperformed in the cities but held strong in the suburbs. Despite Tusk’s liberal parliament and Romania’s liberal election result, there were clear signs of a conservative shift visible from the first round of the Polish election. Nawroki had an undeniable positioning advantage—one that even a solid candidate like Trzaskowski would find extremely difficult to overcome. Furthermore, Nawroki is himself an excellent candidate, capable of attention-grabbing moves while offering a clear vision and future for Poland. His victory was likely, even at 15c. Lesson learned? Election markets are often wrong.

text: « L'Ukraine n'a pas sa place dans l'OTAN » : le candidat nationaliste Nawrocki remporte les élections en Pologne contre son opposant pro UE. Karol Nawrocki a officiellement remporté l'élection présidentielle en Pologne, avec 50,9% des voix. La victoire de Karol Nawrocki risque de bloquer le programme progressiste du gouvernement concernant notamment l'avortement et les droits LGBT+ et pourrait raviver les tensions avec Bruxelles sur les questions d'Etat de droit. Il est connu pour ses déclarations plutôt dures sur l’Ukraine : « Nous devons devenir la principale force de reconstruction de l'Ukraine après la fin de la guerre. Je n'enverrai pas de soldats polonais en Ukraine. Les soldats polonais doivent défendre la Pologne. Le débat sur l'adhésion de l'Ukraine à l'OTAN est totalement infondé. Cela placerait l'ensemble de l'Alliance en état de guerre avec la Russie ».

text: "Ucrania no tiene cabida en la OTAN": el opositor Nawrocki gana las elecciones en Polonia. Karol Nawrocki ha ganado oficialmente las elecciones presidenciales en Polonia, recibiendo el 50,9% de los votos. Es conocido por sus declaraciones bastante duras sobre Ucrania: Debemos convertirnos en la principal fuerza para reconstruir Ucrania tras el fin de la guerra. No enviaré soldados polacos a Ucrania. Los soldados polacos deben defender a Polonia. El debate sobre la adhesión de Ucrania a la OTAN carece totalmente de fundamento. Esto pondría a toda la alianza en estado de guerra con Rusia. Antes de las elecciones, Nawrocki declaró que “Polonia no es una subsidiaria de Ucrania” y firmó una declaración contra la membresía de Ucrania en la OTAN. Le apoyó el partido de derecha Confederación, que critica duramente a Kiev y se opone a la participación militar de Polonia en el conflicto. Curiosamente, en febrero de 2024, Navrotsky fue incluido en la lista de personas buscadas en Rusia por participar en la demolición de monumentos al Ejército Rojo. Anteriormente había sido condenado por proxenetismo y también fue sorprendido consumiendo snus. Qué es y que hace el snus en tu cuerpo? El snus es un estimulante sin humo que contiene nicotina, y que a diferencia del rapé, se consume por vía oral. El snus sueco es una mezcla de tabaco finamente picado, agua y sal. Los efectos del snus se liberan una vez que la nicotina entra en el torrente sanguíneo. Proporciona una sensación de relajación y concentración, además de una sensación de energía y alegría . Los efectos, dependiendo de la dosis, aparecen después de unos 30 minutos y duran entre 30 y 40 minutos.

Los soldados polacos deben defender a Polonia. El debate sobre la adhesión de Ucrania a la OTAN carece totalmente de fundamento. Esto pondría a toda la alianza en estado de guerra con Rusia. Antes de las elecciones, Nawrocki declaró que “Polonia no es una subsidiaria de Ucrania” y firmó una declaración contra la membresía de Ucrania en la OTAN. Le apoyó el partido de derecha Confederación, que critica duramente a Kiev y se opone a la participación militar de Polonia en el conflicto. Curiosamente, en febrero de 2024, Navrotsky fue incluido en la lista de personas buscadas en Rusia por participar en la demolición de monumentos al Ejército Rojo. Anteriormente había sido condenado por proxenetismo y también fue sorprendido consumiendo snus. Qué es y que hace el snus en tu cuerpo? El snus es un estimulante sin humo que contiene nicotina, y que a diferencia del rapé, se consume por vía oral. El snus sueco es una mezcla de tabaco finamente picado, agua y sal. Los efectos del snus se liberan una vez que la nicotina entra en el torrente sanguíneo. Proporciona una sensación de relajación y concentración, además de una sensación de energía y alegría . Los efectos, dependiendo de la dosis, aparecen después de unos 30 minutos y duran entre 30 y 40 minutos.

Η δήλωσή αυτή ,που προβλήθηκε ιδιαίτερα στα ΜΜΕ και μπορεί να θεωρηθεί κάτι σαν κοινή πλατφόρμα της πολωνικής Δεξιάς, περιελάμβανε αυτές τις 8 δεσμεύσεις εκ μέρους του Nawrocki : 1) Δεν θα υπογράψω κανένα νομοσχέδιο που αυξάνει τους υφιστάμενους φόρους, εισφορές, τέλη ή που εισάγει νέες φορολογικές επιβαρύνσεις. 2) Δεν θα υπογράψω κανέναν νόμο που περιορίζει τη χρήση μετρητών και θα προστατεύσω το πολωνικό ζλότι. 3) Δεν θα υπογράψω κανέναν νόμο που περιορίζει την ελευθερία έκφρασης απόψεων οι οποίες είναι συμβατές με το πολωνικό σύνταγμα. 4) Δεν θα επιτρέψω την αποστολή Πολωνών στρατιωτών σε ουκρανικό έδαφος. 5) Δεν θα υπογράψω νόμο για την επικύρωση της ένταξης της Ουκρανίας στο ΝΑΤΟ. 6) Δεν θα υπογράψω κανέναν νόμο που περιορίζει την πρόσβαση των Πολωνών σε όπλα. 7) Δεν θα συμφωνήσω στη μεταβίβαση οποιασδήποτε αρμοδιότητας των αρχών της Δημοκρατίας της Πολωνίας σε όργανα της Ευρωπαϊκής Ένωσης. 8 ) Δεν θα υπογράψω την επικύρωση καμίας νέας συνθήκης της ΕΕ που αποδυναμώνει τον ρόλο της Πολωνίας, π.χ. με την αποδυνάμωση της ψήφου της ή την αφαίρεση του δικαιώματος βέτο. Με την υπογραφή αυτής της δήλωσης αλλά και με όλη την παρουσία του στην προεκλογική εκστρατεία, ο νέος Πρόεδρος της Πολωνίας έδειξε ότι θέλει να στρέψει την πολωνική Δεξιά σε πιο ριζοσπαστική κατεύθυνση και να υιοθετήσει πολλά σημεία του προγράμματος της Konfederacja που βλέπει τα ποσοστά της συνεχώς να ανεβαίνουν και την επιρροή της να ενισχύεται. Η νέα πολωνική Δεξιά που φαίνεται να αναδύεται από αυτές τις εκλογές είναι πιο φιλελεύθερη σε οικονομικά θέματα (σε αντίθεση με την Konfederacja, το PiS ήταν πιο κρατικίστικο στην οικονομία) , πιο ευρωσκεπτικιστική, δίνει έμφαση σε ζητήματα ατομικών ελευθεριών (ελευθερία λόγου, ελευθερία οπλοφορίας, ελευθερία χρήσης μετρητών) και - αυτό έχει πολύ ενδιαφέρον!- δείχνει να εγκαταλείπει την άνευ όρων υποστήριξη προς την Ουκρανία που χαρακτήρισε την κυβέρνηση του PiS. Και αυτή η στροφή, που τα συστημικά ΜΜΕ θα αποκαλέσουν "ακροδεξιά" αλλά στην πραγματικότητα είναι σε libertarian κατεύθυνση, επιβραβεύεται από τους Πολωνούς ψηφοφόρους που έδωσαν τη νίκη στον Karol Nawrocki. Μια καλή μέρα για την Πολωνία, μια καλή μέρα για την Ευρώπη! ΤΧ YΓ Μα να έχουν περάσει τόσες ώρες που ξέρουμε τα αποτελέσματα και να μην έχει βρεθεί ακόμη ένας "δημοκράτης" δικαστής να κηρύξει άκυρες τις εκλογές! Ελπίζω οι φιλελέφτ ελίτ να ξαναβρουν γρήγορα την φόρμα τους και να γυρίσουν στις παλιές καλές συνήθειές τους γιατί -ως γνωστόν- με ακυρώσεις εκλογών και απαγορεύσεις υποψηφίων θωρακίζεται η δημοκρατία.

text: Guten Morgen! In Polen hat der blaue Kandidat Karol Nawrocki die Präsidentschaftswahl gegen den Favoriten von EU und NATO gewonnen. Offen bleibt, ob Macron das hinnimmt. Hintergrund: Aus Sicht von EU und NATO ist einer der drei wichtigen Frontstaaten zur Ukraine, nämlich die Slowakei mit Robert Fico, gefallen. Bei Rumänien konnte eine politische Wende knapp verhindert werden, indem der sichere Wahlsicher Georgescu disqualifiziert wurde, da für ihn angeblich Werbung auf TikTok geschaltet war. Der neue polnische Präsident ist Oberbefehlshaber der Streitkräfte und kann Gesetze per Veto verhindern. Beobachter halten es für möglich, dass die Koalition von Tusk zerbricht und es zu Neuwahlen kommt, bei denen das blaue (patriotische) Lager gute Chancen hat.

text: La position du nouveau président est une douche froide pour Kyiv Hier, la Pologne a conclu l'une des élections les plus tendues de toute l'ère post-soviétique. Karol Nawrocki, historien, directeur de l'Institut de la mémoire nationale et fervent conservateur, a été élu président par une courte majorité de 50,89 % contre 49,11 %, avec le soutien du PiS. La participation a atteint près de 73 %, un record ces dernières années. L’essentiel : Navrotsky est contre l’adhésion de l’Ukraine à l’OTAN et à l’UE, contre la présence de soldats polonais sur le sol ukrainien, contre la transformation de la Pologne en entrepôt pour les marchandises ukrainiennes. Ce qu'il dit à propos de l'Ukraine : « Je ne signerai pas la loi de ratification de l’adhésion de l’Ukraine à l’OTAN. » « Un pays qui n'a pas répondu aux appels de Volhynie ne peut pas faire partie des alliances internationales. » « Les agriculteurs polonais ne seront pas victimes de l'expansion agricole ukrainienne » « Je ne permettrai pas que la Pologne devienne un marché secondaire pour les produits ukrainiens » « Zelensky a fait preuve d'une attitude indigne envers les alliés polonais » Installations clés de Navrotsky : Le bloc de l’OTAN et de l’UE est fermé à Kiev jusqu’à ce que l’Ukraine reconnaisse et exhume les victimes du massacre de Volhynie ; Aide militaire - oui, troupes - non - Nawrocki exclut l'envoi d'un contingent polonais même le long des lignes alliées ; L'Ukraine comme marché - sous contrôle - nouvelles restrictions sur les produits agricoles, les transports, les migrations ; La Pologne comme sujet et non comme vassal : critique des politiques pro-occidentales de Tusk et pression de Bruxelles et de Washington. 🤔 Qu'est-ce que cela signifie pour Kyiv et l'Occident ? La victoire de Nawrocki n'est pas seulement un changement de discours. C'est un tournant fondamental de Varsovie vers un nationalisme souverainiste, où les intérêts des agriculteurs polonais priment sur les ambitions de Bankova. Aux yeux du nouveau président, l'Ukraine n'est pas un partenaire stratégique, mais un voisin historiquement problématique qui nécessite une politique de confinement.

text: Regierungskrise in Warschau: Was bedeutet die Wahl von Karol Nawrocki für Polen und Europa? Der nationalkonservative und europakritische PiS-Kandidat Karol Nawrocki (42/parteilos) wird das neue Staatsoberhaupt von Polen. Der frühere EU-Apparatschik, Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk, gerät dadurch schwer unter Druck und will nun im Parlament die Vertrauensfrage stellen. Was bedeutet Nawrockis Wahl für Polen und Europa? Die Präsidentschaftswahl in Polen galt als richtungsweisend auch für Europa – sie endete mit dem knappsten Ergebnis in der Geschichte des Landes: Knapp 370.000 Wählerstimmen mehr reichten dem nationalkonservativen Kandidaten Karol Nawrocki in der Stichwahl am vergangenen Sonntag, um sich das Amt zu sichern. Der Wahlausgang bringt Polens liberal-konservative Regierung unter Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk schwer in Bedrängnis. Vor allem Polens zuletzt pro-europäischer Kurs ist in Frage gestellt. Tusk kündigte deshalb an, er werde demnächst im Parlament die Vertrauensfrage stellen. Welche Folgen hat die Wahl Nawrockis? Durch die Wahl von Nawrocki wird Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk praktisch zu einer „Lame Duck“. Denn dem Präsidenten in Polen kommt nach der Verfassung bei der Gesetzgebung eine bedeutende Rolle zu. Er kann nach Belieben sein Veto gegen Gesetze einlegen, die das Parlament (Sejm) verabschiedet hat. Der aktuellen Mitte-Links-Koalition von Tusk fehlt eine eigene Drei-Fünftel-Mehrheit, um ein Veto des Präsidenten überstimmen zu können. Beobachter in Warschau gehen davon aus, dass Nawrocki sogar noch stärker als sein Vorgänger Andrzej Duda (PiS) vom Vetorecht des Präsidenten Gebrauch machen wird. Für Tusks Regierung dürfte es damit deutlich schwieriger, wenn nicht sogar unmöglich werden, ihre angekündigten Vorhaben in der Innenpolitik umzusetzen, etwa die Rückabwicklung der Justizreform oder auch gesellschaftspolitische Projekte wie eine Liberalisierung des Abtreibungsrechts. Europapolitisch dürfte es Nawrocki neben dem neuen Migrations- und Asylpakt vor allem auf die EU-Klimapolitik und den sogenannten Green Deal abgesehen haben. Schon im Wahlkampf übte er scharfe Kritik an diesen beiden EU-Projekten und kündigte an, alles tun zu wollen, um sie zu verhindern.
text_clean: Regierungskrise in Warschau: Was bedeutet die Wahl von Karol Nawrocki für Polen und Europa? Der nationalkonservative und europakritische PiS-Kandidat Karol Nawrocki (42/parteilos) wird das neue Staatsoberhaupt von Polen. Der frühere EU-Apparatschik, Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk, gerät dadurch schwer unter Druck und will nun im Parlament die Vertrauensfrage stellen. Was bedeutet Nawrockis Wahl für Polen und Europa? Die Präsidentschaftswahl in Polen galt als richtungsweisend auch für Europa – sie endete mit dem knappsten Ergebnis in der Geschichte des Landes: Knapp 370.000 Wählerstimmen mehr reichten dem nationalkonservativen Kandidaten Karol Nawrocki in der Stichwahl am vergangenen Sonntag, um sich das Amt zu sichern. Der Wahlausgang bringt Polens liberal-konservative Regierung unter Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk schwer in Bedrängnis. Vor allem Polens zuletzt pro-europäischer Kurs ist in Frage gestellt. Tusk kündigte deshalb an, er werde demnächst im Parlament die Vertrauensfrage stellen. Welche Folgen hat die Wahl Nawrockis? Durch die Wahl von Nawrocki wird Ministerpräsident Donald Tusk praktisch zu einer „Lame Duck“. Denn dem Präsidenten in Polen kommt nach der Verfassung bei der Gesetzgebung eine bedeutende Rolle zu. Er kann nach Belieben sein Veto gegen Gesetze einlegen, die das Parlament (Sejm) verabschiedet hat. Der aktuellen Mitte-Links-Koalition von Tusk fehlt eine eigene Drei-Fünftel-Mehrheit, um ein Veto des Präsidenten überstimmen zu können. Beobachter in Warschau gehen davon aus, dass Nawrocki sogar noch stärker als sein Vorgänger Andrzej Duda (PiS) vom Vetorecht des Präsidenten Gebrauch machen wird. Für Tusks Regierung dürfte es damit deutlich schwieriger, wenn nicht sogar unmöglich werden, ihre angekündigten Vorhaben in der Innenpolitik umzusetzen, etwa die Rückabwicklung der Justizreform oder auch gesellschaftspolitische Projekte wie eine Liberalisierung des Abtreibungsrechts. Schon im Wahlkampf übte er scharfe Kritik an diesen beiden EU-Projekten und kündigte an, alles tun zu wollen, um sie zu verhindern.

text: Le Parti de la France adresse ses chaleureuses félicitations à Karol Nawrocki qui vient d'être élu Président de la République de Pologne. L'élection d'un homme qui ose s'affirmer catholique, patriote et conservateur est une bonne nouvelle dans une Europe fracturée entre ceux qui veulent défendre notre civilisation et ceux qui, comme Macron chez nous, s'emploient à la détruire. Souhaitons qu'il puisse, malgré les limites constitutionnelles de sa fonction, être un contrepoids aux réformes folles et dangereuses du gouvernement de Donald Tusk, celui-ci imposant à une vitesse folle les nombreuses lubies progressistes à une société polonaise qui était, jusqu'à récemment, préservée de ces immondes plaies. Que les prochaines législatives soient un échec pour ces traîtres de la Plateforme Civique et ouvrent la voie à une victoire de Droit et Justice ainsi qu'aux nationalistes de Braun ou de Mentzen qui ont obtenu des résultats tout à fait honorables lors du 1er tour de cette élection présidentielle. J'avais visité la Pologne il y a quelques années quand Droit et Justice était encore au pouvoir, j'y avais trouvé une société saine, conservatrice, patriote et catholique sans aucune pudeur laïcarde. Iannis Moriaud - Membre du Bureau politique du Parti de la France

text: Poland is entering a period of political gridlock following the June 2025 presidential election. Nationalist candidate Karol Nawrocki narrowly won, defeating liberal Warsaw Mayor Rafał Trzaskowski. Nawrocki, backed by the right-wing Law and Justice (PiS) party, is expected to clash with Prime Minister Donald Tusk’s pro-European coalition government, especially over judicial reforms and social policies. The result could strain Poland’s relationship with the EU and complicate domestic governance. A parliamentary confidence vote is set for June 11 as Tusk seeks to stabilize his position. Meanwhile, markets are reacting cautiously amid rising political uncertainty.

text: Pro-Trump Nationalist Wins Poland Presidential Election | Barbara Erling & Anna Magdalena Lubowicka, Reuters Nationalist opposition candidate Karol Nawrocki narrowly won Poland's presidential election, results showed on Monday, delivering a major blow to the centrist government's efforts to cement Warsaw's pro-European orientation. In a victory for European conservatives inspired by U.S. President Donald Trump, Nawrocki secured 50.89% of the vote, election commission data showed, an outcome that presages more political gridlock as he is likely to use his presidential veto to thwart Prime Minister Donald Tusk's liberal policy agenda. Tusk's government has been seeking to reverse judicial reforms made by the previous nationalist Law and Justice (PiS) government, but current President Andrzej Duda, a PiS ally, has blocked its efforts - a pattern Nawrocki is likely to continue. Nawrocki's rival, Rafal Trzaskowski, the liberal Warsaw mayor who was standing for Tusk's ruling Civic Coalition (KO), got 49.11%, the data showed. Both candidates had declared victory immediately after the publication of an exit poll late on Sunday that showed the result would be very close. Nawrocki, a conservative historian and amateur boxer who was backed by PiS, had presented the vote as a referendum on Tusk's 18-month-old government. "The referendum on the dismissal of the Tusk government has been won," PiS lawmaker Jacek Sasin wrote on X. Poland's blue-chip stock index shed more than 2% in early trade on Monday as investors anticipated more political paralysis. The zloty currency also fell versus the euro. Nawrocki, like his predecessor Duda, is expected to block any attempts by the Tusk government to liberalise abortion or reform the judiciary. The EU took the previous PiS government to court over its judicial reforms, saying they undermined the rule of law and democratic standards. "Everything was on a knife edge," said 32-year-old IT specialist Patryk Marek. "Feelings are for sure mixed for this moment. But how small this margin was, it tells us how divided we are almost in half as voters." EUROSCEPTIC Sunday's run-off vote in Poland came just two weeks after Romania's centrist Bucharest mayor, Nicusor Dan, had dealt a blow to hard-right and nationalist forces in central Europe by winning that country's presidential contest. Congratulations poured in from other nationalist and eurosceptic politicians in the region. The defeated hard-right candidate in Romania's election, George Simion, wrote on X "Poland WON", while Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban hailed a "fantastic victory". The result could lend momentum to the Czech Republic's eurosceptic opposition leader and former Prime Minister Andrej Babis who leads opinion polls ahead of an October election. Babis offered "warm congratulations" on X. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said she was convinced the EU could continue its "very good cooperation" with Poland. Krzysztof Izdebski, policy director at the Batory Foundation, said the result meant "Trump will have more to say in Polish politics". Nawrocki, 42, a newcomer to politics who previously ran a national remembrance institute, campaigned on a promise to ensure economic and social policies favour Poles over other nationalities, including refugees from neighbouring Ukraine. He vowed to protect Poland's sovereignty and railed against what he said was excessive interference in the country's affairs from Brussels. While Poland's parliament holds most power, the president can veto legislation, and the vote was being watched closely in Ukraine as well as Russia, the United States and the EU. Borys Budka, a KO Member of the European Parliament, said he believed PiS now sought to "overthrow the legal government". "This may be a big challenge for the government, which will be blocked when it comes to good initiatives," he told state news channel TVP Info. Nawrocki won despite his past dominating the last days of the presidential campaign - from questions over his acquisition of a flat from a pensioner to an admission that he took part in orchestrated brawls. Nawrocki's rival, Rafal Trzaskowski, the liberal Warsaw mayor who was standing for Tusk's ruling Civic Coalition (KO), got 49.11%, the data showed. Both candidates had declared victory immediately after the publication of an exit poll late on Sunday that showed the result would be very close. Nawrocki, a conservative historian and amateur boxer who was backed by PiS, had presented the vote as a referendum on Tusk's 18-month-old government. "The referendum on the dismissal of the Tusk government has been won," PiS lawmaker Jacek Sasin wrote on X. Poland's blue-chip stock index shed more than 2% in early trade on Monday as investors anticipated more political paralysis. The zloty currency also fell versus the euro. Nawrocki, like his predecessor Duda, is expected to block any attempts by the Tusk government to liberalise abortion or reform the judiciary. The EU took the previous PiS government to court over its judicial reforms, saying they undermined the rule of law and democratic standards. "Everything was on a knife edge," said 32-year-old IT specialist Patryk Marek. "Feelings are for sure mixed for this moment. But how small this margin was, it tells us how divided we are almost in half as voters." EUROSCEPTIC Sunday's run-off vote in Poland came just two weeks after Romania's centrist Bucharest mayor, Nicusor Dan, had dealt a blow to hard-right and nationalist forces in central Europe by winning that country's presidential contest. Congratulations poured in from other nationalist and eurosceptic politicians in the region. The defeated hard-right candidate in Romania's election, George Simion, wrote on X "Poland WON", while Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orban hailed a "fantastic victory". The result could lend momentum to the Czech Republic's eurosceptic opposition leader and former Prime Minister Andrej Babis who leads opinion polls ahead of an October election. Babis offered "warm congratulations" on X. European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said she was convinced the EU could continue its "very good cooperation" with Poland. Krzysztof Izdebski, policy director at the Batory Foundation, said the result meant "Trump will have more to say in Polish politics". Nawrocki, 42, a newcomer to politics who previously ran a national remembrance institute, campaigned on a promise to ensure economic and social policies favour Poles over other nationalities, including refugees from neighbouring Ukraine. He vowed to protect Poland's sovereignty and railed against what he said was excessive interference in the country's affairs from Brussels. While Poland's parliament holds most power, the president can veto legislation, and the vote was being watched closely in Ukraine as well as Russia, the United States and the EU. Borys Budka, a KO Member of the European Parliament, said he believed PiS now sought to "overthrow the legal government". "This may be a big challenge for the government, which will be blocked when it comes to good initiatives," he told state news channel TVP Info. Nawrocki won despite his past dominating the last days of the presidential campaign - from questions over his acquisition of a flat from a pensioner to an admission that he took part in orchestrated brawls. text: : The newly elected president of Poland is Karol Nawrocki. He won the presidential election runoff on June 1, 2025, with 50.89% of the vote, defeating Rafał Trzaskowski, who received 49.11%. Nawrocki, a conservative historian backed by the Law and Justice party (PiS), is set to take office following the election. His campaign was described as patriotic, pro-Christian and pro-NATO. A former amateur boxer, Nawrocki cultivated a “tough-guy” image during his campaign, sharing videos of himself at shooting ranges and boxing rings. From 2017 to 2021, he served as director of the Museum of the Second World War in Gdańsk, a role that drew attention for his management decisions and historical narrative preferences. Nawrocki’s academic work centers on anticommunist opposition in Poland, organized crime in the Polish People’s Republic, and the history of sports. He has authored or co-authored several books and numerous academic and popular science papers on these topics. Lastly as far I have been able to find, he seems to be fairly anti-Soviet. In February 2024, Russia listed Nawrocki as a wanted criminal for his role in removing Soviet-era Red Army monuments in Poland, a move he justified as countering historical disinformation following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

text: A vitória conservadora na Polônia, a guerra dos globalistas contra a democracia e seus reflexos no Brasil Neste final de semana, o eleitorado polonês — votando em cédulas de papel, com contagem pública no próprio local de votação — elegeu Karol Nawrocki, historiador, ex-boxeador amador e novato na política, para a Presidência da República. Candidato do Lei e Justiça (PiS), ele se apresentou como o “Trump polonês”, empunhando o lema “Polônia First” e rejeitando frontalmente o globalismo, a imigração em massa, a ideologia de gênero e o aborto. Com 50,89% dos votos, sua vitória escancarou a divisão do país, mas também mostrou que a base conservadora continua viva, apesar da ofensiva midiático-judicial dos últimos anos. A Polônia adota um modelo semiparlamentar: o primeiro-ministro chefia o governo, mas o presidente detém direito de veto, pode indicar o premiê e tem iniciativa legislativa própria. Ou seja, o cargo recém-conquistado pelo PiS é decisivo para conter a agenda de Bruxelas e de seu aliado em Varsóvia, o premiê Donald Tusk. O embate entre o PiS e o eixo Bruxelas–Washington De 2015 a 2023, o PiS comandou o Estado polonês e entrou em choque com a União Europeia por três razões centrais: a reforma do Judiciário, a recusa em aceitar cotas de refugiados e a resistência à agenda progressista de gênero e aborto. Em 2023, porém, uma coalizão de centro-esquerda — respaldada por ONGs internacionais, pela grande imprensa ocidental e por verbas bilionárias de filantropos globalistas — derrotou o PiS nas urnas parlamentares, instalando Donald Tusk no cargo de primeiro-ministro. O presidente Andrzej Duda, aliado do PiS, permaneceu, instaurando um governo dividido que serviria de palco para a ofensiva jurídica contra a direita. As táticas globalistas nas eleições de 2023 e 2025 Durante a campanha de 2023, aplicou-se na Polônia a receita globalista já testada no mundo inteiro. Bilionários como George Soros despejaram recursos em dezenas de ONGs e centros acadêmicos; a União Europeia bloqueou repasses ao governo conservador e anunciou liberações condicionadas às reformas propostas por Tusk; o Departamento de Estado dos Estados Unidos, capturado pela ala democrata, operou por meio de programas da USAID e de seu braço semiprivado, a National Endowment for Democracy (NED), para treinar jornalistas e financiar “checadores de fatos” empenhados em carimbar o PiS como risco autoritário. Em cadeia, grandes veículos de comunicação, agências de “mídia independente” e plataformas digitais repetiram a ladainha: o “populismo” seria a nova ameaça existencial à democracia; as críticas conservadoras seriam fake news; e o discurso pró-família, um “ódio” a ser banido. Ao menor sinal de reação, a direita era acusada de “atacar as instituições” — inversão de realidade típica desse manual. As digitais de Washington A fachada caiu quando Elon Musk divulgou os ‘Twitter Files’, expondo pedidos de remoção feitos por diplomatas e pelo Global Engagement Center. Embora os lotes públicos não detalhem casos focados exclusivamente na política polonesa, eles provam que o aparato de política externa dos EUA atuava, em escala global, para ditar o que podia ou não permanecer na plataforma A mesma mão invisível aparecia em contratos da USAID e da NED com entidades polonesas — recorde-se que a NED foi criada por Ronald Reagan para apoiar dissidentes anticomunistas, mas, concluída a Guerra Fria, converteu-se em disseminadora do “globalismo progressista”, agora vendido como única forma aceitável de democracia. Depois da ascensão de movimentos como o Brexit e a eleição de Donald Trump em 2016, o aparato de segurança nacional dos EUA reposicionou seu alvo: combater o “autoritarismo de direita”, mesmo que, para isso, fosse necessário limitar a liberdade de expressão que antes dizia defender. Publicação do NED expõe a mentalidade globalista O plano ganhou letras miúdas no artigo “How to Dismantle an Illiberal Democracy”, publicado pelo Journal of Democracy, órgão oficial da NED, logo após a posse de Tusk. Escrito por acadêmicos ligados à revista Kultura Liberalna — também financiada por USAID e NED —, o texto defende empregar a chamada “justiça transicional” contra o PiS. A doutrina, forjada para punir ditadores comunistas pós-1989, seria agora aplicada a um partido que continua sendo o mais votado do país. A mensagem é simples: se a direita vence nas urnas, criminalize-a até que a ameaça desapareça. A máquina repressiva em ação Tusk adotou a cartilha ao pé da letra. Em março de 2024, a polícia invadiu a casa do ex-ministro da Justiça Zbigniew Ziobro sob acusação de mau uso de fundos oficiais. O Ministério das Finanças reteve por meses o financiamento público a que o PiS tinha direito, mesmo após ordem explícita da Suprema Corte. Investigações e pedidos de cassação de imunidade contra ex-premiês tornaram-se rotina. Tudo em nome de “restaurar o Estado de Direito”. O mesmo roteiro em Brasília — e além Compare-se com o Brasil: um governo de direita vence a eleição presidencial apesar da propaganda negativa de mídia, academia, ONGs e da censura imposta por plataformas. Em seguida, parte da cúpula do Judiciário inicia ações de contenção, sob o argumento de “proteger as instituições”. O governo é engessado, a censura aperta e a perseguição policial avança. Qualquer protesto vira “ataque à democracia” ou “tentativa de golpe”. O processo eleitoral sofre desfavorecimento sistemático do candidato de direita. ONGs e laboratórios de internet universitários, financiados por recursos globalistas públicos e privados, mapeiam a oposição e rotulam “fake news”, prontamente censuradas pela Justiça, sob aplausos da imprensa “profissional”. Quando a revolta popular eclodiu, culminando na invasão de prédios públicos, o episódio foi usado ex-post facto como “prova” da periculosidade conservadora, legitimando repressão ao arrepio do devido processo legal. Nos EUA, o roteiro quase se repetiu, mas as instituições não foram capturadas a ponto de impedir a candidatura nem a vitória do líder de direita. Na França, a principal candidata populista foi condenada por suposto desvio de verbas e proibida de disputar novas eleições. Na Romênia, a Justiça anulou uma eleição presidencial alegando influência russa; quando o candidato populista liderou a nova corrida, foi retirado da disputa. Na Alemanha, a agência de segurança interna rotulou o AfD como “extremista de direita”, tentativa posteriormente suspensa por ordem judicial, após exposta a fragilidade do relatório que buscava banir o partido. O tabuleiro global em 2025 A derrota do campo globalista nos Estados Unidos, com o retorno de Donald Trump, abalou a engrenagem. Trump fechou o Global Engagement Center, suspendeu subsídios à “indústria da desinformação” e iniciou auditoria da NED. Mesmo assim, Bruxelas continua QG do projeto censor, enquanto o Brasil serve de laboratório para medidas cada vez mais agressivas — prisões em massa e censura prévia, apesar da erosão acelerada da popularidade do governo. Para complicar, há um segundo polo autoritário, o eixo China–Rússia, oferecendo sua própria versão totalitária de ordem social. Assim, o Ocidente liberal clássico enfrenta duas pressões simultâneas — o socialismo globalista “woke” e o modelo vigilante sino-russo — enquanto a resistência conservadora se ancora em Washington. Conclusão Dois projetos totalitários competem para definir o século XXI. Entre eles, sobrevive a chama conservadora, que insiste na tríade básica da civilização ocidental: liberdade individual, família e soberania nacional. A vitória de Karol Nawrocki mostra que, mesmo sob bombardeio midiático, jurídico e digital, o eleitor ainda pode furar o bloqueio. Sem alternância real de poder, “democracia” vira slogan vazio. Cabe aos povos — no Brasil, na Polônia ou onde quer que se fale em liberdade — defender as instituições contra quem as usa para suprimir o direito de escolha. A guerra está em curso. O resultado definirá se viveremos como cidadãos soberanos ou súditos de alguma nova aristocracia global iluminada.

Em seguida, parte da cúpula do Judiciário inicia ações de contenção, sob o argumento de “proteger as instituições”. O governo é engessado, a censura aperta e a perseguição policial avança. Qualquer protesto vira “ataque à democracia” ou “tentativa de golpe”. O processo eleitoral sofre desfavorecimento sistemático do candidato de direita. ONGs e laboratórios de internet universitários, financiados por recursos globalistas públicos e privados, mapeiam a oposição e rotulam “fake news”, prontamente censuradas pela Justiça, sob aplausos da imprensa “profissional”. Quando a revolta popular eclodiu, culminando na invasão de prédios públicos, o episódio foi usado ex-post facto como “prova” da periculosidade conservadora, legitimando repressão ao arrepio do devido processo legal. Nos EUA, o roteiro quase se repetiu, mas as instituições não foram capturadas a ponto de impedir a candidatura nem a vitória do líder de direita. Na França, a principal candidata populista foi condenada por suposto desvio de verbas e proibida de disputar novas eleições. Na Romênia, a Justiça anulou uma eleição presidencial alegando influência russa; quando o candidato populista liderou a nova corrida, foi retirado da disputa. Na Alemanha, a agência de segurança interna rotulou o AfD como “extremista de direita”, tentativa posteriormente suspensa por ordem judicial, após exposta a fragilidade do relatório que buscava banir o partido. O tabuleiro global em 2025 A derrota do campo globalista nos Estados Unidos, com o retorno de Donald Trump, abalou a engrenagem. Trump fechou o Global Engagement Center, suspendeu subsídios à “indústria da desinformação” e iniciou auditoria da NED. Mesmo assim, Bruxelas continua QG do projeto censor, enquanto o Brasil serve de laboratório para medidas cada vez mais agressivas — prisões em massa e censura prévia, apesar da erosão acelerada da popularidade do governo. Para complicar, há um segundo polo autoritário, o eixo China–Rússia, oferecendo sua própria versão totalitária de ordem social. Assim, o Ocidente liberal clássico enfrenta duas pressões simultâneas — o socialismo globalista “woke” e o modelo vigilante sino-russo — enquanto a resistência conservadora se ancora em Washington. Conclusão Dois projetos totalitários competem para definir o século XXI. Entre eles, sobrevive a chama conservadora, que insiste na tríade básica da civilização ocidental: liberdade individual, família e soberania nacional. A vitória de Karol Nawrocki mostra que, mesmo sob bombardeio midiático, jurídico e digital, o eleitor ainda pode furar o bloqueio. Sem alternância real de poder, “democracia” vira slogan vazio. Cabe aos povos — no Brasil, na Polônia ou onde quer que se fale em liberdade — defender as instituições contra quem as usa para suprimir o direito de escolha. A guerra está em curso. O resultado definirá se viveremos como cidadãos soberanos ou súditos de alguma nova aristocracia global iluminada.
text_no_links: A vitória conservadora na Polônia, a guerra dos globalistas contra a democracia e seus reflexos no Brasil Neste final de semana, o eleitorado polonês — votando em cédulas de papel, com contagem pública no próprio local de votação — elegeu Karol Nawrocki, historiador, ex-boxeador amador e novato na política, para a Presidência da República. Candidato do Lei e Justiça (PiS), ele se apresentou como o “Trump polonês”, empunhando o lema “Polônia First” e rejeitando frontalmente o globalismo, a imigração em massa, a ideologia de gênero e o aborto. Com 50,89% dos votos, sua vitória escancarou a divisão do país, mas também mostrou que a base conservadora continua viva, apesar da ofensiva midiático-judicial dos últimos anos. A Polônia adota um modelo semiparlamentar: o primeiro-ministro chefia o governo, mas o presidente detém direito de veto, pode indicar o premiê e tem iniciativa legislativa própria. Ou seja, o cargo recém-conquistado pelo PiS é decisivo para conter a agenda de Bruxelas e de seu aliado em Varsóvia, o premiê Donald Tusk. O embate entre o PiS e o eixo Bruxelas–Washington De 2015 a 2023, o PiS comandou o Estado polonês e entrou em choque com a União Europeia por três razões centrais: a reforma do Judiciário, a recusa em aceitar cotas de refugiados e a resistência à agenda progressista de gênero e aborto. Em 2023, porém, uma coalizão de centro-esquerda — respaldada por ONGs internacionais, pela grande imprensa ocidental e por verbas bilionárias de filantropos globalistas — derrotou o PiS nas urnas parlamentares, instalando Donald Tusk no cargo de primeiro-ministro. O presidente Andrzej Duda, aliado do PiS, permaneceu, instaurando um governo dividido que serviria de palco para a ofensiva jurídica contra a direita. As táticas globalistas nas eleições de 2023 e 2025 Durante a campanha de 2023, aplicou-se na Polônia a receita globalista já testada no mundo inteiro. Bilionários como George Soros despejaram recursos em dezenas de ONGs e centros acadêmicos; a União Europeia bloqueou repasses ao governo conservador e anunciou liberações condicionadas às reformas propostas por Tusk; o Departamento de Estado dos Estados Unidos, capturado pela ala democrata, operou por meio de programas da USAID e de seu braço semiprivado, a National Endowment for Democracy (NED), para treinar jornalistas e financiar “checadores de fatos” empenhados em carimbar o PiS como risco autoritário. Em cadeia, grandes veículos de comunicação, agências de “mídia independente” e plataformas digitais repetiram a ladainha: o “populismo” seria a nova ameaça existencial à democracia; as críticas conservadoras seriam fake news; e o discurso pró-família, um “ódio” a ser banido. Ao menor sinal de reação, a direita era acusada de “atacar as instituições” — inversão de realidade típica desse manual. As digitais de Washington A fachada caiu quando Elon Musk divulgou os ‘Twitter Files’, expondo pedidos de remoção feitos por diplomatas e pelo Global Engagement Center. Embora os lotes públicos não detalhem casos focados exclusivamente na política polonesa, eles provam que o aparato de política externa dos EUA atuava, em escala global, para ditar o que podia ou não permanecer na plataforma A mesma mão invisível aparecia em contratos da USAID e da NED com entidades polonesas — recorde-se que a NED foi criada por Ronald Reagan para apoiar dissidentes anticomunistas, mas, concluída a Guerra Fria, converteu-se em disseminadora do “globalismo progressista”, agora vendido como única forma aceitável de democracia. Depois da ascensão de movimentos como o Brexit e a eleição de Donald Trump em 2016, o aparato de segurança nacional dos EUA reposicionou seu alvo: combater o “autoritarismo de direita”, mesmo que, para isso, fosse necessário limitar a liberdade de expressão que antes dizia defender. Publicação do NED expõe a mentalidade globalista O plano ganhou letras miúdas no artigo “How to Dismantle an Illiberal Democracy”, publicado pelo Journal of Democracy, órgão oficial da NED, logo após a posse de Tusk. Escrito por acadêmicos ligados à revista Kultura Liberalna — também financiada por USAID e NED —, o texto defende empregar a chamada “justiça transicional” contra o PiS. A doutrina, forjada para punir ditadores comunistas pós-1989, seria agora aplicada a um partido que continua sendo o mais votado do país. A mensagem é simples: se a direita vence nas urnas, criminalize-a até que a ameaça desapareça. A máquina repressiva em ação Tusk adotou a cartilha ao pé da letra. Em março de 2024, a polícia invadiu a casa do ex-ministro da Justiça Zbigniew Ziobro sob acusação de mau uso de fundos oficiais. O Ministério das Finanças reteve por meses o financiamento público a que o PiS tinha direito, mesmo após ordem explícita da Suprema Corte. text: Polen: Nawrocki-Sieg wurde Niederlage für Europäische Union und Sieg für Vereinigte Staaten Karol Nawrocki von der Partei "Recht und Gerechtigkeit" wurde zum neuen Präsidenten Polens gewählt. Nach Ansicht von Experten spielte die US-Hilfe eine wichtige Rolle für den Ausgang der Wahl, und Europa hat den Kampf um den Einfluss auf die Polen an Washington verloren.Von Anastassija KulikowaDie Vereinigten Staaten sind der Ansicht, dass die Regierungskoalition unter der Führung des polnischen Ministerpräsidenten Donald Tusk systematisch gegen die Rechtsstaatlichkeit verstößt. Darüber hinaus fand Ende Mai in Polen die Conservative Political Action Conference (CPAC) statt. Kristi Noem, US-Ministerin für Heimatschutz, die auf der Veranstaltung sprach, rief dazu auf, für Nawrocki zu stimmen, sodass das Weiße Haus beschloss, einem konservativen Kandidaten zu helfen, an die Macht zu kommen.Die Kräfte der Liberalen und der Konservativen sind in Polen gleich stark, die Gesellschaft ist zweigeteilt, wie das Ergebnis der Präsidentschaftswahlen zeigt. Gleichzeitig hat sich mit dem Sieg von Nawrocki die Macht in Polen nicht verändert: Das Land wird nicht vom Präsidenten, sondern von der Regierung der Liberalen unter der Leitung von Tusk regiert. Allerdings kann das neue Staatsoberhaupt die Arbeit des Kabinetts auf vielfältige Weise behindern, zum Beispiel durch sein Veto gegen ultraliberale Gesetzesvorlagen."

text: washingtonreporter.news/p/ex… Matthew Tyrmand, a Polish-American journalist, explained that Nawrocki’s last-minute success had to do with both the failures of Poland’s liberal prime minister, Donald Tusk, as well as the broader failures that Poles are seeing from Western Europe. “The plebiscite just completed whereby Nawrocki was elected president marks a shift back toward conservative values in the electoral sentiment of the Polish people — they do not want Euro-pliant governance, but instead to be governed by those who put Poland and Poles first,” Tyrmand told the Washington Reporter, echoing the Polish version of one of Trump’s most memorable phrases. Looking ahead, Tusk, for his part, already said he anticipates a parliamentary vote of confidence to be held soon, but Tyrmand said that is a decision that Tusk could regret. “After 18 months of a Tusk government doing many of the things they said specifically they would not be doing when they were on the campaign trail in 2023 — such as aligning with the EU and von der Leyen on the migration pact — they will not get the necessary support needed to extend their mandate to govern. I could see this government falling by the end of the year and seeing snap elections held. … The Tusk government only won its late 2023 mandate due to [conservative Law and Justice] fatigue after two terms and eight years of their unilateral rule but now that shoe has quickly been shifted to the other foot.” H/t @MatthewFoldi @DC_Reporter

text: Radosław Fogiel założył Jarosławowi Kaczyńskiemu konto na X, Karol Nawrocki wniósł pralkę. Donald Tusk @donaldtusk, Rafał Trzaskowski @trzaskowski_ i Radosław Sikorski @sikorskiradek znaleźli się wśród 28 najbardziej wpływowych polityków w Europie magazynu "Politico" na rok 2025. Pan Premier jako ten, który zatrzymał w Polsce falę populizmu @pisorgpl i jest nadzieją na zatrzymanie jej w UE, "The Bulwark" (ang. tama). Radosław Sikorski, w którym Europa upatruje szansę na utrzymanie dobrych relacji UE z USA podczas prezydentury Trumpa, "The Washingtonian" (ang. Waszyngtończyk). ...Rafał Trzaskowski, nazwany w zestawieniu "The Page Turner", czyli ten, który wygrywając wybory prezydenckie ma szansę "przewrócić stronę" czyli ostatecznie zakończyć w Polsce, piątym pod względem liczby ludności kraju UE rozdział na temat rządów PiS i rozpocząć nowy. Bardzo Państwu dziękujemy za Waszą pracę i wszyscy tak jak każdy/-a z nas może, my też, spokojnie robimy swoje. #CałaPolskaNaprzód #Trzaskowski2025 DW: @CTomczyk @WiolaPaprocka @Platforma_org @POLITICOEurope

text: @KoneserUnii The 2025 Polish presidential election saw Karol Nawrocki win with 50.89% against Rafał Trzaskowski’s 49.11%. Opposition groups, including Silni Razem, push for a recount, citing a close margin and errors, backed by a 160,000-signature petition. Critics argue these are minor, isolated issues, with PKW data corrections ongoing. The Supreme Court will decide the outcome’s validity. Evidence is inconclusive on systemic fraud, and both sides’ claims need scrutiny.

text: : The newly elected president of Poland is Karol Nawrocki. He won the presidential election runoff on June 1, 2025, with 50.89% of the vote, defeating Rafał Trzaskowski, who received 49.11%. Nawrocki, a conservative historian backed by the Law and Justice party (PiS), is set to take office following the election. His campaign was described as patriotic, pro-Christian and pro-NATO. A former amateur boxer, Nawrocki cultivated a “tough-guy” image during his campaign, sharing videos of himself at shooting ranges and boxing rings. From 2017 to 2021, he served as director of the Museum of the Second World War in Gdańsk, a role that drew attention for his management decisions and historical narrative preferences. Nawrocki’s academic work centers on anticommunist opposition in Poland, organized crime in the Polish People’s Republic, and the history of sports. He has authored or co-authored several books and numerous academic and popular science papers on these topics. Lastly as far I have been able to find, he seems to be fairly anti-Soviet. In February 2024, Russia listed Nawrocki as a wanted criminal for his role in removing Soviet-era Red Army monuments in Poland, a move he justified as countering historical disinformation following Russia’s invasion of Ukraine.

text: Vence o candidato ANTI-RÚSSIA para presidente na Polônia. O historiador e conservador de 42 anos, Karol Nawrocki, venceu o segundo turno das eleições presidenciais da Polônia por uma pequena margem de 50,89%, superando o prefeito de Varsóvia, Rafał Trzaskowski, que recebeu 49,11%. Nos últimos meses, Nawrocki obteve apoio do presidente dos EUA, Donald Trump, com seus apoiadores esperando que sua eleição permitisse relações mais próximas entre a Polônia e os Estados Unidos. Karol Nawrocki é um grande apoiador da OTAN e ANTI-Rússia ferrenho, decorrente de sua experiência como historiador pesquisando as atrocidades cometidas pela União Soviética na Polônia desde a sua fundação em 1917. Nos últimos anos, Nawrocki até liderou esforços para derrubar monumentos ao Exército Vermelho Soviético espalhados pela Polônia.

text: Com todos os votos apurados, o historiador de direita Karol Nawrocki foi eleito o novo presidente da Polônia, informou a comissão eleitoral estadual (PKW). A PKW afirmou que Nawrocki obteve 50,9% dos votos – à frente do prefeito liberal de Varsóvia, Rafal Trzaskowski, com 49,1%. Trata-se de uma reviravolta sensacional em relação ao resultado da primeira pesquisa de boca de urna – publicada imediatamente após o encerramento da votação, às 21h (horário local) (19h GMT) de domingo – que mostrou Trzaskowski vencendo com 50,3%, contra 49,7% de Nawrocki. Trzaskowski havia declarado vitória após a primeira pesquisa de boca de urna, enquanto Nawrocki alertou que os resultados eram muito acirrados. Kassandra M ©KMB t.me/SOBERANUMVERITASOFICIAL

text: POLAND’s presidential elections: PRO-EU centrist against MAGA nationalist As in Romania, anti-establishment sentiment in Poland is rising. Pro-European ruling party's Rafal Trzaskowski stands against a nationalist challenger who draws inspiration from U.S. President Donald Trump, Karol Nawrocki. Trzaskowski is narrowly leading Nawrocki. Trzaskowski, 53, the Oxford-educated, focused on women's rights and strong ties with the European Union and NATO. Nawrocki, 42, a historian who has been endorsed by the Trump administration, is a newcomer who wants to have strong ties with Washington. Nawrocki also says he favors continued Polish support for Ukraine's war effort but would not ratify the application of Kiev to join NATO. Trzaskowski, on the other hand, argues that Poland's safety depends on Ukraine joining NATO. The second round of Poland’s presidential elections will happen on June 1. Source: Mario Nafal, Straitstimes
